mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition occurs but SPX 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, prompting market repricing of near-term earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production declines in late-cycle regimes, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of stable or falling federal funds rates alongside monthly inflation readings below 2.5% creates a favorable real yield environment, encouraging equity allocation and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified monetary-inflation combination fails to generate positive 20d returns in > 58% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth signals an economic recession, immediately triggering risk-off sentiment and capital flight from equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days of data release.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 55% of quarters following negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in event-study analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with accelerating retail sales during a post-shock recovery phase indicates successful supply chain normalization and demand fulfillment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint supply-demand expansion condition yields negative 20d returns in > 60% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy falls below 51% in out-of-sample tests.
